dimensions.name: Extractability
dimensions.desc: Can machines extract the text (vs scanned image / encrypted / corrupt)

dimensions.name: Chunkability
dimensions.desc: Document length and semantic boundaries suit chunking

dimensions.name: Redundancy
dimensions.desc: Exact / near duplicate ratio, version sprawl

dimensions.name: Freshness
dimensions.desc: Recency of content, share of stale files

findings.text: 20% of files yield no machine-extractable text (scanned image / encrypted / corrupt). What the model cannot read does not exist.
2 likely scanned-image PDFs detected. Without OCR, these are invisible to the AI.
About 14% of content is duplicate / near-duplicate. Duplicates make retrieval return conflicting versions.
3 files are too small to carry context on their own.
About 40% of files are over two years old. Stale content is the main source of 'statistically relevant but factually wrong'.

stat.label: Expected RAG failure rate on current data (rough estimate)

rows.label: Estimated data-prep workload (rough, not a quote)

cta.title: Next step: from check-up to delivery
cta.body: Your data readiness is Yellow: a base exists but with clear gaps. Fixing data before investing in AI sharply lowers failure rates.
